source.definition: to have life or exist; to be alive

explanation.sourceText: The English word "live" comes from the Old English "libban" or "lifan", which originally meant to remain alive or to continue existence.
explanation.targetText: Cuvântul englezesc „live“ provine din engleza veche „libban“ sau „lifan“, care inițial însemna a rămâne în viață sau a continua existența.

source.additional: An irregular verb used in many contexts, from describing physical life to emotional and mental states.

target.definition: a avea existență sau a fi în viață

explanation.sourceText: The Romanian verb „a trăi“ has its roots in the Latin „vivere“, meaning "to live", showing its connection to the Romance languages.
explanation.targetText: Verbul românesc „a trăi“ provine din latină, de la „vivere“, care înseamnă „a trăi“, reflectând legătura cu limbile romanice.

target.additional: Regular verb of the 3rd conjugation (-i). It is used to express both physical life and emotional experience.

examples.sourceText: I have lived here a long time.
They used to live in this city.
examples.targetText: Ei trăiau înainte în acest oraș.

examples.sourceText: I would live there if I could.
examples.targetText: Aș trăi acolo dacă aș putea.

learningTips.text: Remember that "a trăi" sounds like "a try" in English, so think of "trying to live" as a way to recall the verb.
The Romanian verb "a trăi" is related to the English word "trial" which also comes from Latin roots, helping you connect the idea of life as a journey or test.
Think of the English phrase "to live and let live" to remember the meaning of "a trăi" as living life fully.
The ending "-i" in "a trăi" is typical for Romanian verbs of the third conjugation, similar to English infinitives ending in "-e" like "live".
